9.reason: [The variable i is reinitialized to 0 on every iteration of the while loop, so the stack pointer never advances. This causes all pushes to overwrite the same position and all pops to read from the same index, leading to incorrect output.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Error: The variable i is reinitialized to 0 on every iteration of the while loop, so it never tracks the actual stack top across multiple operations. It should be declared outside the loop.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  'i' and 'j' are declared inside the loop, causing the stack pointer to reset each iteration. It should be declared outside the loop (e.g., before while).

This causes all push/pop operations to behave as if the stack is always empty or at index 0.]

16.reason: [The condition i>=0 is always true, but after a pop, i should be decremented before accessing the array. Here, i is used as the index before decrementing, so it accesses the wrong element (the next empty slot or out-of-bounds). Also, the condition should check if i>0 to avoid underflow.]
